cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. So Jennifer went to the swimming pool today, and I need to figure out between which times she could have gone. The answer options are given as A to D. Let me go through the information step by step. First, the facts given: - Jennifer woke up at 5am. - Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. - Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. - Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. - Sarah saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. - Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. - The swimming pool was closed after 10pm. So the question is: Between what times could Jennifer have gone to the swimming pool? Let me break this down. To find out when Jennifer could have gone to the pool, I need to figure out the time windows when she wasn't doing any of the other activities that were reported by Richard, Samantha, Betty, Sarah, or Thomas. Also, the pool is closed after 10pm, so she must have gone before that. Let me list all the times she was seen doing other things: - 5am to 8am: working at the office - 12pm to 1pm: reading at the library - 1pm to 2pm: buying a bike - 2pm to 9pm: buying a phone - 9pm to 10pm: walking to the Statue of Liberty So her schedule is: 5am to 8am: office Then, there's a gap from 8am to 12pm. Could she have gone to the pool then? Well, the pool is open until 10pm, so 8am to 12pm is possible if there's no other activity during that time. Then from 12pm to 1pm: library.
